plan.grooveRootCause: CONFIRMED BY LIVE RUN (vite + agent-browser, ?at=street&r=0.4 and default 2cm start; /tmp/v5-02-street.png, /tmp/v5-03.png, /tmp/v5-04-open2cm.png): the 謎の溝 is the v4 OSM ground ribbon layer (render/osmGround.js, one vertex-colored BatchedMesh) around the opening strip. Three compounding factors: (1) the 総武線 rail corridor maps to z≈0 and its warm-brown ribbon (CLASS_COLOR[5]=0x6b6157) crosses the exit lane immediately east of the curated strip (x>25) — at ball r 0.02–0.5m the multi-track swath (~3–4 game m wide) reads as a giant flat band; (2) dark-grey road ribbons (0x44474f..0x60636a) west/north of the strip sit at full contrast against the brown/grey terrain — the "dark-grey diagonal band next to brown ground" in the owner screenshot; (3) the v4 hotfix clipPolylineOutsideRects (build-tokyo-bin.mjs:204) removes ribbons only INSIDE the shop+strip rects, so ribbons end in hard rectangular cuts exactly at the strip edge — visible as abrupt "trench ends". Additionally the 2cm opening confirmed barren: nearest interior parts are 0.5–0.9m away while fog far = 55r = 1.1m, so the player sees an empty brown floor (feeds requirement 2). FIX (radius-continuous, no data re-run): radius-driven ground-layer fade — osmGround's ground-batch material gets an onBeforeCompile injection mixing the vertex color toward the live terrain ground tint by (1 - smoothstep(FADE_R0, FADE_R1, trueRadius)), FADE_R0=0.6 / FADE_R1=3.0 true-m (module-local constants like GROUND_LIFT_K). trueRadius = radiusSim*worldScale is already plumbed (main.js:883 passes radiusSim as osmGround.update 4th arg) and is invariant under the similarity rescale, so KeyR pixel-identity holds by construction; ribbons are invisible through the whole validated opening and fade in continuously exactly as the band-3 OSM streetscape arrives (r≈1–3m). Hard clip-cuts become invisible at small radius and negligible at the radius where ribbons appear. One uniform write per frame (zero alloc), draw calls unchanged, terrain tint read via a new env.getGroundColorWorking(target) getter (palette-crossfade aware).
plan.anchorDecision: KEEP the OSM anchor at (35.69870, 139.76930); theme-only Sengoku rename. Verified numerically against scripts/osm/geo.mjs projection math: the proposed real-千石電商 anchor (35.6993, 139.7713) is only 193m real (≈39 game m) from the current anchor, but under it the real ラジオ会館 (35.69842, 139.77133) reconciles to game (0.5, 19.5) — INSIDE the frozen shop+strip exclusion and directly ON the opening play lane. The dioramaR-24 ラジオ会館風ビル landmark plus its r=28.8 exclusion circle would sit on the validated opening street, destroying the byte-identical opening-minute gate, and would force: full pipeline re-fetch/re-convert, regeneration of all 12 OSM_GEN reconciliation values + pasted cross-check constants, exclusion re-bake, EXPECTED_COUNTS drift, navigability re-gate, and Phase-3 pacing re-runs — all for ~39 game m of geographic difference. Decisive bonus: under the CURRENT anchor the real 千石電商 location maps to game (36.2, -13.3), i.e. ~36 game m from the shop door — theming the existing shop as センゴク電子 is already geographically honest without moving anything. Renames only: tiers.js T0 name 'パーツ棚'→'センゴク電子' (HUD progress label), catalog.js code-92 displayNameJa 'アキバパーツ館'→'センゴク電子' (light parody name, legally safe), donackLines.js 'start' line. SHOP geometry/exclusions/pipeline data: untouched.
plan.spec:
V5 BINDING MINI-SPEC (polish pass; all v3/v4 invariants survive: rescale pixel-identity, seamlessness/radius-continuity, zero per-frame alloc, draw cap 72, deterministic spawning, JS gz 240KB, OSM data budget untouched — NO pipeline re-run anywhere in v5).

[1] 謎の溝 FIX — radius-driven OSM ground fade (Stream C).
Files: src/render/osmGround.js, src/render/environment.js. Root cause: see grooveRootCause. Implementation: ground-batch MeshBasicMaterial gets onBeforeCompile uniform pair uGroundFade01/uGroundTint; fragment: color.rgb = mix(uGroundTint, vColor, uGroundFade01). Per-frame (inside existing update(dt, ballPos, fogFarSim, radiusSim) — 4th arg already passed at main.js:883): fade = smoothstep(OSM_GROUND_FADE_R0=0.6, OSM_GROUND_FADE_R1=3.0, radiusSim*scaleMgr.worldScale); uGroundTint copied from new env.getGroundColorWorking(targetColor) (environment.js adds this getter, palette-crossfade aware). Constants module-local (NOT tuning.js). Water mesh excluded (env material, rivers are far). Gates: KeyR force-rescale screenshot diff at r=0.5/1.5/4 (mid-fade) — trueRadius is rescale-invariant so diff must be zero; agent-browser opening run shows clean floor at 2cm–60cm; ribbons fully in by r=3m.

[2] OPENING ONBOARDING (Streams A + B-copy).
Files: src/config/cityMap.js, src/game/onboarding.js (NEW), src/config/donackLines.js.
(a) Spawn carpet: new V5_OPENING_CLUSTERS (~5 rows: screw/resistor/led/capacitor/button_battery, centers ringing (0,0), spread 0.5–0.9, n≈8 each, r 0.006–0.011, ySurf 0) so first absorb lands <2s in any roll direction. (b) Breadcrumb trail: V5_TRAIL_CLUSTERS (~3 rows: screw/led/paperclip, rect-clamped {x0:0.6,x1:4.4,z0:-0.3,z1:0.3}, n≈8 each) carpeting the interior exit lane; it connects to the existing frozen exterior gutter/exit-lane carpet. DETERMINISM LAW: all V5 cluster rows expand via a SEPARATE rng stream rng2=mulberry32(0x56355041) appended AFTER the v4 expansion block — every existing v4 placement stays byte-identical (no opening regression risk from rng-stream shift). Validator: re-check full-clear window 0.28..0.58 (ΣR³ delta ≈ +3e-5 m³, negligible), 3D-reach on new rows, PLACEMENTS ≤ CURATED_PLACEMENT_CAP.
(c) Parts-guide arrow: NEW src/game/onboarding.js — class Onboarding(bus, camera, scaleMgr): emits EVT.GOAL_GUIDE at 10Hz (reusing the existing HUD #goal-arrow path verbatim, projection copied from finale._projectAndEmitGuide, module scratch Vector3, zero alloc) pointing at the nearest unconsumed waypoint of a static list [(1.5,0,-1.0) interior bins, (5.0,0,0) gate, (11,0,0) gutter carpet] in REAL meters converted via worldScale each emit. Active iff score==0 AND elapsed<ONBOARD_MAX_S=30 AND finale.state=='idle'; one final {active:false} emit on first ABSORB or timeout; reset() rearms on resetWorld. No collision with finale's GOAL_GUIDE by construction (finale emits only from APPROACH at r≥420m) — DEV assert both never active same frame. main.js (integrator): construct after finale, call onboarding.update(frameDt) at step 4.6, resetWorld += onboarding.reset().
(d) Donack: donackLines.js 'start' line text → 「センゴク電子パーツからスタート！光る矢印の先にパーツがあるよ」 (covers the req-5 line too).

[3] スタックチャン collectible id 12 (Stream B + placement row in Stream A's cityMap.js).
Files: src/world/objects.js + src/config/catalog.js + src/render/ball.js (move together), src/game/collection.js, src/ui/screens.js, src/config/tuning.js, index.html; placement in src/config/cityMap.js.
Codes: collectible code=70+id rule CANNOT extend (code 82 = 西郷さん像) — v5 codes append after the 110-entry table: objects.js exports V5_CODE_BASE=110, V5_ARCHETYPE_IDS=['stack_chan'(110),'game_center'(111),'denki_retailer'(112),'maid_cafe'(113),'pc_parts_bldg'(114)], ARCHETYPE_ID_BY_CODE length assert 110→115, and export collectibleCodeForId(id) = id<=11 ? 70+id : V5_CODE_BASE+(id-12). knockOff skip >= EXTRA_CODE_BASE(70) already covers 110+ (permanently stuck — verified in ball.js, plus its 110 cross-check assert → 115 and stuck-family mapping for 110..114).
catalog.js: addExtra(110,'collectible-small',{id:'stack_chan', displayNameJa:'スタックチャン', naturalBand:1, radiusNominal:0.04, spawnWeight:0, upright:true, ≤350 tris}) — cute voxel cube: body box, dark screen-face front panel inset, 2 cyan eye quads (vertex colors), tiny side-arm stubs; M5Stack open-source robot tribute, "Donack's cousin". DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE + CATALOG asserts 110→115. EXTRA_POOL_CAPS['collectible-small'] 12→13 (now 12 member archetypes; cap = worst concurrent).
cityMap.js: COLLECTIBLE_IDS.STACK_CHAN=12 (append-only enum); COLLECTIBLES += {id:12, nameJa:'スタックチャン', x:0.065, y:0.7, z:-2.6, radiusReal:0.04, archetypeCode:110, landmarkId:-1, naturalBand:1, rIntent:0.4} (shelf-A mid level, same SHELF_X convention as existing y0.7 items — an electronics shop shelf is its home). Validator: COLLECTIBLES.length 13, colCount[13], code rule via collectibleCodeForId, archetypeCode range assert (cityMap.js:888) widened from <=92 to (<=92 || (>=110 && <=114)).
collection.js: nameForId + prerenderThumbnails switch from EXTRA_CODE_BASE+id to collectibleCodeForId(id); KNOWN_MASK=(1<<COLLECT_TOTAL)-1 auto-extends; APPEND-ONLY mask compat is structural (unknown-high-bit preservation already implemented) — test: hand-seed v4 {v:1,mask:0xFFF} in LS, assert 12/13 found and bit 12 collectable.
tuning.js: COLLECT_TOTAL 12→13. screens.js grid loop + result text n/13 + X share text all derive from COLLECT_TOTAL (no further edit). index.html CSS: #collection-grid stays repeat(4,56px) (48px mobile); add .cell:nth-child(13){grid-column:2/4; justify-self:center} so the 13th cell centers on row 4 — mobile fit preserved (4 rows × 56px).

[4] AKIBA curated buildings (Stream B archetypes + Stream A placements).
catalog.js: 4 new EXTRA-style archetypes, codes 111..114, vertex-colored primitives ≤350 tris each, naturalBand 4, spawnWeight 0, sizeClass 'landmark-mid': game_center 'ゲームセンター' (radiusNominal 8 — bright multicolor signage bands + marquee strip), denki_retailer '家電量販店' (14 — tall colorful banded facade, red/white/yellow floors), maid_cafe 'メイドカフェ' (6 — cream box, pink awning + accent bands), pc_parts_bldg 'PCパーツショップビル' (9 — zakkyo with green/blue parts-shop signage rows). EXTRA_POOL_CAPS['landmark-mid'] 4→12 (worst co-location: 6 akiba placements + ラジオ会館 + headroom; capacity raise only — same batch, zero extra draws; boot feasibility assert updated).
cityMap.js: V5_AKIBA_CLUSTERS — 6 singleton rows (n=1, spread 0) in the 電気街 zone east of the strip, expanded via the same v5 rng2 stream: game_center (32,-60) r 8 and (44,95) r 7.5, denki_retailer (58,30) r 14, maid_cafe (30,12) r 6, pc_parts_bldg (36,-25) r 9 and (66,-105) r 10; rIntent = radiusReal/0.65 (absorbable at ball r≈9–22m = mid-game T3/T4). landmarkId -1, collectibleId -1, FLAG_CURATED via normal curated path. Possible interpenetration with OSM footprints is accepted hakoniwa style; reviewed via ?at=street&r=8 teleport screenshot, positions are single-constant nudges.

[5] SENGOKU shop theming — anchor KEPT (decision + evidence in anchorDecision). Edits: tiers.js T0 name 'パーツ棚'→'センゴク電子' (drives HUD #tier-label); catalog.js code 92 displayNameJa →'センゴク電子'; donackLines.js 'start' line (see [2d]). Comments referencing アキバパーツ館 updated opportunistically; SHOP geometry, exclusions, OSM bins: zero change.

[6] SPACE-EARTH ENDING (Stream C).
Files: src/game/finale.js, src/render/earthView.js (NEW), src/render/environment.js; tuning.js GOAL_ASCEND_S 5.0→7.0 (single constant, executed by Stream B as tuning.js owner).
earthView.js (moon-precedent sky element: fog:false, frustumCulled:false, NOT part of world rescale — only ever visible post-contact where rescale/rebase cannot fire): (1) Earth sphere mesh ~48×32 segs (~3k tris, boot-built once): procedural vertex colors via mulberry32(0x45415254)-seeded 3-octave value noise — dark ocean 0x06101e, faint continent patches 0x16261c, city-light speckles 0xffd27a on land verts with density boost in a Japan-arc region; twinkle via one onBeforeCompile uTime uniform modulating a per-vertex aTwinkle attribute (zero per-frame alloc — one uniform write). (2) Star dome: THREE.Points ~700 pts on a far shell, same uTime twinkle. NO textures, no postprocessing. API: show()/hide(), setProgress01(u) (fade-in + parallax sink of the Earth below the anchor), setAnchor(x,y,z) called by finale per frame, setTime(t). Draws: +2, finale-only → worst 70 ≤ cap 72 (ledger note added).
finale.js: ASCENSION u∈[0,1] now reads as liftoff — CINE_BACK_K ramps 14→CINE_BACK_END_K=30 and CINE_UP_K 4→CINE_UP_END_K=8 over u (module-local consts), look target biases downward late (lerp toward anchor-(0,6r,0) for u>0.5) so the glowing Earth fills the lower frame while the ball+Skytree silhouette rises; at ascension start: existing env.beginNightFade(GOAL_ASCEND_S) + earthView.show() + new env.setSpaceFade01(u) per frame (environment.js: one uniform darkening the sky-dome zenith toward space black — radius-independent, finale-only). AFTERGLOW: earthView stays, glow breathing unchanged. skipCinematic SKIP_TIME_SCALE=5 untouched (full sequence 1.2+7.0+2.5=10.7s → ~2.1s skipped); state machine order, GAME_WIN emission, result handoff, reset() (+= earthView.hide()) unchanged. Donack win line text gains 「うわぁ…地球が見えるよ…！」 (donackLines.js, Stream A).
Late-wire: finale.setEarthView(v) hook (setEffects precedent) so the frozen 7-arg constructor survives; main.js (integrator) constructs EarthView and injects.

INTEGRATION (lead, main.js only): construct Onboarding + EarthView, wire step-4.6 onboarding.update, finale.setEarthView, resetWorld += onboarding.reset(). Order B → A → C (B lands codes/asserts first; A's cityMap row references code 110; C independent). Full pass: boot asserts green (115/115 everywhere), validateCityMap green, opening agent-browser run (2cm: parts visible <2s, arrow live, no ribbons), KeyR diff at r=0.5/1.5/4, ?at=street&r=8 akiba review, ?at=goal finale run + skip path, LS mask compat test, gz budget ≤240KB. Docs-site/GitHub: outside this workflow.

checkProblems.title: SHOWSTOPPER: extraPools.js render-side CLASS_CAPS is the hard capacity authority and NO stream owns the file — both new caps overflow it
checkProblems.fix: src/render/extraPools.js:371 `const CLASS_CAPS = Object.freeze([12, 6, 6, 6])` is what BatchedExtraPool is actually constructed with (line 397); EXTRA_POOL_CAPS in catalog.js is only the documented spec floor. The plan raises EXTRA_POOL_CAPS collectible-small 12→13 and landmark-mid 4→12, but the render pools stay at 12 and 6: 13 collectible-small archetypes can be concurrently live at T5/T6 map-wide load radii (13 > 12), and landmark-mid worst case becomes the audited 5 existing (codes 80,82,83,84,86) + 6 akiba placements = 11 (11 > 6). The plan only says 'watch the extraPools.js line-365 mirror' in risks but extraPools.js appears in no stream's file list — an executor following the binding spec never edits it. Fix: add src/render/extraPools.js to Stream B (it owns the capacity decision) or Stream C (render owner); change CLASS_CAPS to [13, 12, 6, 6], update the capacity-audit comment at lines 364-370, and extend whatever DEV feasibility check exists to compare CLASS_CAPS >= worst-case concurrency so this can never silently regress.

checkProblems.title: SHOWSTOPPER: screens.js hardcodes the code=70+id rule for collection-grid cell names — plan spec [3] wrongly says 'no further edit' for screens.js
checkProblems.fix: src/ui/screens.js:465 inside _buildCollectionGrid: `const nameJa = DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE[EXTRA_CODE_BASE + id]`. For id=12 this resolves to DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE[82] = '西郷さん像', so the スタックチャン cell renders the Saigo statue's name. The plan is internally contradictory: spec [3] states 'screens.js grid loop + result text n/13 + X share text all derive from COLLECT_TOTAL (no further edit)' while the risks section says screens.js must route through collectibleCodeForId — a Stream B executor following the binding spec text will skip the edit. Fix: amend spec [3] to explicitly include the screens.js:465 change to `DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE[collectibleCodeForId(id)]` (import from objects.js), and add a DEV assert or test that walks ids 0..COLLECT_TOTAL-1 verifying every display-name lookup resolves to a defined, collectible-class code so any future hardcoded 70+id reintroduction throws at boot.

checkProblems.title: Spec bug: 13th-cell CSS selector targets a class that does not exist — grid cells are .collect-cell, not .cell
checkProblems.fix: The plan's index.html rule `.cell:nth-child(13){grid-column:2/4; justify-self:center}` matches nothing: screens.js:455 creates cells with `cell.className = 'collect-cell'` ('.cell-new' is only the NEW badge span inside a cell). The 13th cell would sit left-aligned in column 1 of row 4 instead of centered. Fix: use `#collection-grid .collect-cell:nth-child(13){grid-column:2/4; justify-self:center}` (and mirror under the 48px mobile breakpoint at index.html:727 if widths differ). Verify visually in the win-overlay since #win-overlay.staged (index.html:739) animates the grid wrap.

checkProblems.title: Onboarding arrow reuses #goal-arrow verbatim but the 🗼 glyph is hardcoded in index.html — the parts guide will display a Skytree icon
checkProblems.fix:
index.html:929 `
🗼
` — the glyph is static markup, not part of the EVT.GOAL_GUIDE payload. The plan's own Donack line 「光る矢印の先にパーツがあるよ」 tells the player the arrow points at parts, but for the first 30 seconds it will read as the tower-goal arrow (🗼), actively miscommunicating the finale mechanic it was designed for. Fix: add an optional `glyph` (or `kind:'parts'|'goal'`) field to the GOAL_GUIDE payload; hud._onGoalGuide (hud.js:539) swaps the text node to 🔩 (or similar) when kind==='parts' and restores 🗼 otherwise — a diffed one-line DOM write, zero alloc. The plan's DEV assert that onboarding and finale are never simultaneously active already guarantees no glyph flicker.

streams.approach: cityMap.js: appended a v5 expansion block AFTER the frozen v4 block using a separate rng stream mulberry32(0x56355041) ('V5PA') — V5_OPENING_CLUSTERS (5 rows x 8 T0 parts ringing the 2cm start, spread 0.5-0.6, interior-rejection sampled) + V5_TRAIL_CLUSTERS (3 rows x 8 rect-clamped to the interior exit lane x0.6-4.4 |z|<=0.3) + 6 akiba building singletons (codes 111-114, naturalBand 4, yaw from rng2, rIntent=r/ABSORB_RATIO). スタックチャン added as COLLECTIBLE_IDS.STACK_CHAN=12 + COLLECTIBLES row {x:0.065,y:0.7,z:-2.6,r:0.04,code:110(CODE_STACK_CHAN),band:1,rIntent:0.4} on shelf A; the collectibles loop's interior flag extended to id 12 (inShop = id<=3 || id===12) so the 3D-reach/elevated validators apply. Validator updated: code range (<=92 || 110..114), 13 defs, code rule asserted through objects.js collectibleCodeForId (single mapping authority), colCount[13], plus a new v5 block asserting per-code placement counts {110:1,111:2,112:1,113:1,114:2}, strip-edge clearance (x-r>25) and ラジオ会館風ビル disc clearance (d >= dioramaR24+r) for every akiba building. onboarding.js (NEW): Onboarding(bus, camera, scaleMgr, finale=null) + setFinale late-wire; 10Hz EVT.GOAL_GUIDE emitter reusing the finale projection verbatim (module scratch Vector3, no-catch-up throttle, skip-one-tick on RESCALE); points at ordered waypoints [(1.5,-1.0) bins, (5,0) gate, (11,0) gutter] authored in REAL meters, converted via live worldScale per emit, consumed via camera-as-ball-proxy distance; active iff no ABSORB yet AND elapsed<30s AND finale idle; one final {active:false} emit (only if ever shown), hard-deactivates on REBASE (conversion validity) or non-idle finale, DEV throw on guide collision, self-rearms on GAME_START plus reset() for main.resetWorld. Payload gains kind:'parts' while live, restored to 'goal' on retirement so finale emits (which never write kind) stay correct on the shared payload. donackLines.js: 'start' retexted to センゴク電子+arrow callout, 'ascension' retexted to the earth line, col_stackchan appended + COLLECT_LINE_IDS[12], stale entry-count comments corrected (45 entries). Verified: node --check all three; full validateCityMap green (520/640 placements, full-clear 0.540m within 0.28-0.58, growth-chain unchanged); v4 byte-identity proven (all 449 pre-change placements value+order identical vs /tmp/v4-placements-baseline.json); opening density: nearest absorbable part 0.035m from spawn, 14 within 0.6m, 34 parts on the trail lane; Onboarding lifecycle harness green (10Hz emits, absorb/timeout retirement, rearm).
keyDecisions: Akiba building positions nudged from the plan's draft constants (single-constant nudges the plan authorizes): game_center (32,-60)->(34,-60), denki_retailer (58,30)->(66,60), maid_cafe (30,12)->(32,-8) — the draft coordinates put denki_retailer 15.6m and maid_cafe 18.9m from the ラジオ会館風ビル landmark center (dioramaR 24), i.e. INSIDE the landmark model, and two footprints crossed the curated strip edge x=25. New validator asserts make both clearances structural so future nudges can't regress.

Validator code rule routed through objects.js collectibleCodeForId (imported) rather than a local formula — objects.js stays the single mapping authority and a Stream-B drift throws at module load (Stream B's objects.js had already landed when I verified, so the import resolves).

id-12 placement flows through the existing COLLECTIBLES loop (not the v5 appendix) with the inShop flag extended to id 12 — this shifts landmark/shell array indices by 1, which is safe because curated.js flattens PLACEMENTS into SoA by value (no index-coupled determinism); v4 placement VALUES+relative order proven byte-identical against the pre-change baseline.

Onboarding hard-deactivates permanently on EVT.REBASE: a rebase shifts the sim origin away from the real origin, breaking the real->sim waypoint conversion; rebase cannot fire in the legitimate opening window (ball is meters from origin), so this only affects dev teleports — safer than wrong arrows.

GOAL_GUIDE payload gains a dynamic kind field: 'parts' while the onboarding guide is live, restored to 'goal' on the single retirement emit — since finale never writes kind, the shared payload reads correctly for the entire finale afterward (adversarial-check fix [4], emitter side).

Onboarding waypoint consumption uses the camera position as ball proxy (update(frameDt) signature has no ball arg per the binding plan); camera horizontal offset at opening scale (~0.2 real m) is far below the 1.0-2.0m consume radii.
Onboarding additionally self-rearms on EVT.GAME_START (belt-and-suspenders beside main.resetWorld wiring) so title-screen idle time never consumes the 30s budget.

Donack 'ascension' line retexted IN PLACE (id frozen, append-only contract intact): 「うわぁ…地球が見えるよ…！東京の光、きれいだね」 — covers the space-earth ending; 'start' covers both req-2 arrow callout and req-5 センゴク rename in one line per plan.
Stale donackLines count comment corrected: the table was already 44 entries (the Phase-3 col_golden_objet append never bumped the '43' comment); now documented as 45 with col_stackchan.
streams.notesForIntegration: main.js wiring (integrator-owned): (1) construct `new Onboarding(bus, camera, scaleMgr, finale)` AFTER the finale (or use onboarding.setFinale(finale)); (2) call onboarding.update(frameDt) at frame-order step 4.6, AFTER finale.update so the finale-state gate reads this frame's truth; (3) resetWorld += onboarding.reset(). hud.js + events.js (NOT in my file list — must be picked up by the integrator or Stream B/C per adversarial fix [4]): _onGoalGuide (hud.js:539) must read p.kind — swap the #goal-arrow glyph 🗼->🔩 (diffed one-line DOM write) when kind==='parts' and restore 🗼 otherwise, AND gate the 「スカイツリーへ向かえ！」 toast + the _guideToastShown latch on kind!=='parts' — otherwise the toast fires at game start and the once-latch eats the real finale toast later. Recommend adding `kind: 'goal'` to PAYLOADS.goalGuide in events.js (I set the field dynamically from onboarding; pre-declaring keeps the hidden class stable). Dependency state at hand-off: Stream B's objects.js (V5_CODE_BASE/collectibleCodeForId/codes 110-114), tiers.js センゴク電子 rename, catalog.js v5 archetypes and tuning.js COLLECT_TOTAL=13 had ALL already landed when I verified — cityMap.js imports collectibleCodeForId and is green against the live repo (full validateCityMap output clean, 520/640 placements). v4 byte-identity baseline snapshot for re-verification: /tmp/v4-placements-baseline.json (compare PLACEMENTS minus the contiguous 70-entry tail appendix minus the collectibleId===12 row). Visual review still pending per plan: ?at=street&r=8 teleport screenshot of the 6 akiba buildings (positions are single-constant nudges in V5_AKIBA_CLUSTERS, cityMap.js; the validator now enforces strip-edge and radio-kaikan clearance so nudges can't silently regress) and the 2cm opening agent-browser run (parts <2s + arrow live). Onboarding gate ordering note: if a dev teleport (?at=goal) starts with score 0, onboarding retires silently on its first update via the finale-state gate (no active emit ever fires, so no {active:false} blip — _everEmitted gates the off-emit).

streams.approach: Implemented the v5 code-table extension (110->115), the スタックチャン collectible (id 12, code 110), the 4 Akihabara curated archetypes (codes 111..114), the センゴク電子 re-theme, and the COLLECT_TOTAL 13 / GOAL_ASCEND_S 7.0 tuning constants, honoring all 4 adversarial-check fixes. objects.js: V5_CODE_BASE=110, V5_ARCHETYPE_IDS (5, frozen order), table-population loop, exported collectibleCodeForId(id) (ids 0..11 -> 70+id, id 12+ -> 110+), asserts widened to 115 with a collectibleCodeForId rule assert. catalog.js: stack_chan voxel-cube robot (108 tris — orange cube body, inset dark screen panel, cyan eyes/smile, arm stubs, feet) registered via addExtra(110,'collectible-small'), plus game_center(111, r8)/denki_retailer(112, r14)/maid_cafe(113, r6)/pc_parts_bldg(114, r9) as landmark-mid naturalBand-4 buildings (108-144 tris each, all unit-sphere finish(), spawnWeight 0); EXTRA_POOL_CAPS 13/12/4/4; code-92 displayNameJa -> センゴク電子; DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE builder + dev asserts extended to 115 incl. frozen-name asserts for codes 92/110 and a dedicated v5 assert block. ball.js: V5_STUCK_FAMILY table (110 boxy, 111 boxy, 112 pillar, 113 boxy, 114 pillar) with a three-way family dispatch in attachStuck (v5 codes MUST NOT fall into the OSM branch — out-of-bounds index), asserts 115 + V5 coverage; knockOff >= EXTRA_CODE_BASE skip covers 110+ unchanged. collection.js + screens.js: all id->code lookups routed through collectibleCodeForId (fixing the adversarial showstopper at screens.js:465 where id 12 would have rendered 西郷さん像), plus a collection.js boot assert walking ids 0..COLLECT_TOTAL-1. tuning.js: COLLECT_TOTAL=13, GOAL_ASCEND_S=7.0. tiers.js: T0 name -> センゴク電子. index.html: corrected 13th-cell CSS (#collection-grid .collect-cell:nth-child(13){grid-column:2/4; justify-self:center} — the spec's '.cell' selector matched nothing) and the static #tier-label default text. Verified with node table/geometry tests, Vite SSR DEV-assert loads of all 9 modules (validateCityMap still green), a localStorage mask-compat test (v4 0xFFF -> 12/13, collect id 12 -> 13/13 persisted 0x1FFF, unknown high bits preserved), and a production build (221.30 KB gz <= 240 KB budget).
keyDecisions: Honored adversarial fix #1 by editing src/render/extraPools.js (outside my nominal list, explicitly assigned by the finding): CLASS_CAPS [12,6,6,6] -> [13,12,6,6] plus a new boot DEV assert that CLASS_CAPS[k] >= EXTRA_POOL_CAPS[class] so a future spec-floor raise can never silently overflow the live pools again.

Found and fixed an UNASSIGNED showstopper the adversarial check missed: src/world/curated.js:692 `kind = EXTRA_POOL_CLASS[code - EXTRA_BASE]` indexes a 23-entry Int8Array — v5 codes 110..114 read out of bounds (kind undefined -> no render pool -> every Stream-A v5 placement spawns INVISIBLE but collidable). Added a local V5_POOL_CLASS table (110 -> collectible-small, 111..114 -> landmark-mid, mirroring catalog) and a code >= V5_BASE branch, in the file's existing style with no new imports. No other stream lists curated.js, so no conflict risk.

ball.js family dispatch needed a real code change, not just asserts: the existing `code >= OSM_CODE_BASE` branch would swallow v5 codes and index out of the 16-entry OSM_STUCK_FAMILY. Added V5_STUCK_FAMILY with a three-way ternary; stack_chan folds to the boxy proxy (it IS a cube), buildings to box/pillar per the OSM building-proxy law.

Honored adversarial fixes #2 and #3: screens.js:465 now uses DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE[collectibleCodeForId(id)] (was 70+id -> 西郷さん像 for id 12), with a collection.js boot assert walking every displayed id; index.html uses the real `.collect-cell` class for the centered 13th cell (one width-independent rule covers both the 56px and 48px breakpoints).

Registered v5 codes through the existing addExtra path (EXTRA_CATALOG/EXTRA_SIZE_CLASS_BY_CODE gain keys 110..114): extraPools.js buildExtraPools already scans codes 70..ARCHETYPE_ID_BY_CODE.length-1, so the new archetypes join the 4 shared batches automatically — zero extra draw calls, capacity raises only.

センゴク電子 rename executed as theme-only (anchor KEPT per the binding anchorDecision): tiers.js T0 name (drives HUD #tier-label via the TIER event), catalog code-92 displayNameJa, index.html static #tier-label default (so the pre-first-event HUD matches), plus opportunistic comment updates in tuning.js/objects.js. archetype id 'akiba_parts_shop' kept frozen (ids are append-only contract).

GOAL_ASCEND_S 5.0 -> 7.0 landed here as tuning.js owner per the plan; full finale sequence becomes 1.2+7.0+2.5=10.7s, ~2.1s skipped at SKIP_TIME_SCALE 5 — Stream C's finale/earthView work consumes it unchanged.
streams.notesForIntegration: All 9 touched modules load clean under Vite SSR with DEV asserts firing (115/115 everywhere, validateCityMap still green on v4 data); prod build 221.30 KB gz (budget 240). For Stream A: cityMap.js COLLECTIBLES row for stack_chan must use archetypeCode 110 / id 12 / naturalBand 1 / radiusReal 0.04 (matches catalog radiusNominal), the akiba rows codes 111..114 with radiusReal 8/7.5/14/6/9/10 per the spec, and the cityMap.js:888 archetype-code range assert must be widened to accept 110..114 (I did NOT touch cityMap.js); collection.js will throw at boot if the placement/total drift. For Stream A/integrator: the adversarial #goal-arrow glyph fix (hud.js:539 + EVT.GOAL_GUIDE `kind` field) is NOT done — it belongs with onboarding.js. Out-of-list edits to flag in review: render/extraPools.js (adversarial-mandated CLASS_CAPS [13,12,6,6] + floor assert) and world/curated.js (V5_POOL_CLASS dispatch — without it every v5 curated placement renders invisible; see keyDecisions). LS compat verified by test: v4 {v:1,mask:0xFFF} loads as 12/13, collecting id 12 persists 0x1FFF, unknown high bits pass through. The 13th collection cell centers via #collection-grid .collect-cell:nth-child(13); verify visually in the staged win overlay. HUD #tier-label now defaults to センゴク電子 (6 chars vs 4) — worth one glance on a 360px-wide phone. Donack lines (start + win) are Stream A's donackLines.js edits and are not duplicated here.

streams.approach: [1] 謎の溝 fix (osmGround.js + environment.js): the ground-batch MeshBasicMaterial gets an onBeforeCompile uniform pair (uGroundFade01/uGroundTint held as live objects on the class, linked in the shader hook so writes are valid pre/post compile); fragment injects `diffuseColor.rgb = mix(uGroundTint, diffuseColor.rgb, uGroundFade01)` after <color_fragment>. Per frame inside the existing update() (4th arg radiusSim already passed at main.js:883): fade = smoothstep(OSM_GROUND_FADE_R0=0.6, OSM_GROUND_FADE_R1=3.0) on trueRadius = radiusSim*worldScale (rescale-invariant → KeyR pixel-identity by construction); tint copied from the new env.getGroundColorWorking(target) (live _cGround, palette-crossfade aware). Env reaches osmGround via a NEW setEnvironment(env) late-wire (the v4 constructor signature is frozen); unwired = exact v4 rendering + one loud DEV warn. River/water mesh untouched. [6] Space-earth ending: new render/earthView.js — procedural night Earth (unit 48x32 sphere, 2976 tris, mulberry32(0x45415254)-seeded 3-octave value-noise continents, dark ocean 0x06101e / land 0x16261c / city-light verts 0xffd27a with coastal bias + Japan-arc density boost near the camera-facing pole) + 700-pt star shell; both twinkle via ONE shared uTime uniform modulating a per-vertex aTwinkle(amp,phase) attribute through a common onBeforeCompile injector. Key rendering insight: both meshes are transparent/depthTest:false/depthWrite:false/fog:false so they paint in the transparent pass AFTER the whole opaque world — covering the fully-fogged night diorama below without touching any world mesh — with renderOrder slots blobShadow(1) < stars(4) < earth(5) < effects sparkles(10), giving correct paint-order occlusion (globe hides lower-hemisphere stars and the parked blob shadow; the golden fountain stays in front). environment.js adds setSpaceFade01(k) → uSpaceFade sky uniform darkening the zenith to space black (elevation-weighted; the night-fog horizon band reads as the atmospheric limb), reset by setTierPaletteImmediate. finale.js: setEarthView() late-wire (setEffects precedent), ascension = liftoff (earthView.show() at entry; per frame u drives env.setSpaceFade01(u) + _driveEarth(progress/anchor/clock)); _driveCinematic ramps CINE_BACK_K 14→30 and CINE_UP_K 4→8 over eased dimensionless _liftU with the look target sliding to anchor-(0,6r,0) for u>0.5; afterglow holds u=1; reset() += earthView.hide() + _liftU=0. Skip path, state order, GAME_WIN emission and result handoff untouched. Everything verified in live runs (agent-browser): clean 2cm opening, mid-fade at r=1.5, full ribbons at r=4, KeyR invariance, full unskipped finale with visible glowing Earth, skipped finale, no console errors.
keyDecisions: osmGround needs the Environment for the live ground tint but its 4-arg constructor is frozen and main.js is integrator-owned — added osmGround.setEnvironment(env) late-wire with a safe degradation (unwired = exact v4 rendering + one DEV console.warn so the missing wire is loud in dev)
Fade constants OSM_GROUND_FADE_R0=0.6 / R1=3.0 are module-local in osmGround.js (GROUND_LIFT_K precedent), NOT tuning.js, per the binding mini-spec

KeyR pixel-identity is satisfied to 1 ulp, not bit-exact: trueRadius is recomputed as radiusSim*worldScale, so a forced rescale changes the product by float round-off (measured 0.31640625 vs 0.31640625000000017 at r=1.5 — 1.7e-16, sub-pixel by construction)

EarthView renders in the TRANSPARENT pass with depthTest:false (renderOrder: stars 4, earth 5 — between blobShadow 1 and effects 10): this is what makes 'Earth below the diorama' possible at all, since the opaque ground plane/buildings would otherwise depth-occlude anything placed below y=0; paint-order gives correct star/globe/fountain layering with zero changes to world meshes

EarthView.setAnchor takes a 4th arg r (frozen post-contact ball radius, sim units) — the spec's 3-arg signature had no scale reference; all sizes are dimensionless multiples of r (EARTH_R_K=45, gap 12→30 parallax sink, star shell 160r), worst case 1500/2000 sim inside CAMERA_FAR 4000

Earth geometry/colors boot-built once from mulberry32(0x45415254) (verified bit-identical across two constructions); per-frame cost = 1 shared uTime uniform write + 2 opacity scalars + 2 transforms, zero alloc; 2976 tris + 700 points = +2 finale-only draws (worst ledger 70/72, noted in the header)

finale._liftU is dimensionless (phase fraction) and deliberately NOT in _simCache — documented inline why the rescale/rebase law doesn't apply; camera ramp consts CINE_BACK_END_K=30/CINE_UP_END_K=8/CINE_LOOK_DOWN_K=6 are finale-local

env uSpaceFade is applied BEFORE the star layer in SKY_FRAG so shader stars brighten against true space, and the final horizon fog melt is kept — the night-fog band reads as the planet's atmospheric limb for free

Did NOT touch tuning.js (GOAL_ASCEND_S 5→7 is Stream B's single-constant edit; my code is constant-agnostic and was verified at the current 5.0), did NOT touch main.js, no pipeline/data changes (no osm:build re-run needed)
streams.notesForIntegration: main.js (integrator) needs THREE wiring lines from this stream: (1) REQUIRED for the 謎の溝 fix to activate — `osmGround.setEnvironment(env);` right after the OsmGround construction at main.js:305 (unwired it renders exactly as v4 and logs '[osmGround] setEnvironment(env) not wired — v5 ground fade (謎の溝 fix) inactive' in DEV; this wire was NOT in the original integration list, do not drop it); (2) `import { EarthView } from './render/earthView.js';` + `const earthView = new EarthView(renderer.scene);` (anywhere after renderer, e.g. next to the finale chain at main.js:452); (3) `finale.setEarthView(earthView);` next to the existing finale.setEffects(effects) at main.js:455. NO resetWorld addition needed for earthView — finale.reset() already calls earthView.hide(), and env uSpaceFade is cleared by setTierPaletteImmediate on GAME_RESET (existing reset ownership). Everything was verified live by late-wiring exactly these calls through window.__v3dbg (screenshots /tmp/v5e-*.png finale with Earth, /tmp/v5o-*.png opening/fade radii): clean 2cm floor, fade 0.316 at r=1.5, fade 1 at r=4, KeyR invariant to 1 ulp, full + skipped finale both reach the result screen with no console errors. Bundle: 221.3KB gz before earthView enters the graph; earthView adds ~2.1KB gz (esbuild-measured) → ~223.5KB ≤ 240 budget. Stream B's GOAL_ASCEND_S 5.0→7.0 in tuning.js is still pending (runs above used 5.0; finale/earthView read the constant so no code change needed when it lands). EarthView.setAnchor(x,y,z,r) takes the ball radiusSim as a 4th argument — finale already passes it; if anyone else ever drives EarthView, remember the scale ref. The 90 lit city-light verts / Japan-arc placement is near the sphere's +Y pole because the liftoff camera always sees the globe's upper cap — if a future change views the globe from below, rotate JAPAN_DIR in earthView.js.

fixes: main.js: wired osmGround.setEnvironment(env) right after OsmGround construction (REQUIRED activator for the 謎の溝 radius-fade fix — was logging the DEV 'not wired' warning path otherwise)
main.js: constructed EarthView(renderer.scene) + finale.setEarthView(earthView) next to finale.setEffects (setEffects precedent, frozen 7-arg constructor preserved)

main.js: constructed Onboarding(bus, renderer.camera, scaleMgr, finale) after the finale; onboarding.update(frameDt) inserted at frame-order step 4.6 (after finale.update + GAME_WIN emit, before step 5 ball.update); resetWorld += onboarding.reset(); both added to __v3dbg
src/core/events.js + src/types.js: PAYLOADS.goalGuide gained kind:'goal' (pre-declared field keeps the hidden class stable) + GoalGuideEvent typedef/doc updated

src/ui/hud.js: _onGoalGuide now reads p.kind — diffed one-line text-node write swaps the #goal-arrow glyph 🗼→🔩 for kind:'parts' and restores 🗼 otherwise; the 「スカイツリーへ向かえ！」 toast AND its _guideToastShown once-latch are gated on kind!=='parts' (latch no longer eaten at game start; real finale toast preserved). Cached this._arrowGlyphNode/_arrowKind in the constructor

src/config/cityMap.js: v5 akiba building rows changed colorHex -1 → 0xffffff (BUG FOUND IN VALIDATION: -1 fell back to the curated FALLBACK_PALETTE greys 0x7d8790/0xb5bdc6/... which multiply the vertex colors and washed all 6 facades to dull grey; identity white tint restores the authored rainbow signage / pink awning — verified live before/after)

index.html: #collection-header static total /12 → /13 (BUG FOUND IN VALIDATION: result screen showed 「コレクション 13/12」; screens.js only writes the found-count span, the total was hardcoded) + comment tying it to COLLECT_TOTAL
remainingIssues: '[spawner] chunk record pool exhausted' DEV warnings fire under extreme dev-teleport load (W-held at r≈600m, hundreds of m/s) — graceful skip path, pre-existing chunk-spawner behavior, ZERO occurrences in a clean normal opening run (verified with cleared console); not v5-caused (v5 adds curated placements, not chunk records)

Onboarding parts-guide also activates on dev teleports started with score 0 (e.g. ?at=street) and points back toward the shop exit lane — dev-only cosmetic, retires on first absorb / 30s / waypoint walk as designed

Two synthetic agent-browser runs completed the finale at skip pace without a deliberate skip input; could NOT be reproduced under controlled hands-off conditions (in-page rAF phase logger showed full pace ts=1: merge 1.2s / ascension 7.07s / afterglow 2.53s, twice) — attributed to stray synthetic pointer/key events from the automation harness, recommend one human phone playtest of the ending pacing

agent-browser tab crash mid-session reset the browser profile and wiped localStorage once — environment artifact, NOT a game bug (v4 12-bit mask compat was explicitly re-verified: {v:1,mask:0xFFF} loads as 12/13, collecting id 12 persists 0x1FFF append-only)
validation.narrative: Integration: wired all three pending main.js hooks (osmGround.setEnvironment — the 謎の溝 fix activator that was NOT in the original list, EarthView + finale.setEarthView, Onboarding at step 4.6 + resetWorld) and landed the adversarial hud.js/events.js GOAL_GUIDE kind fix that neither stream owned. Boot asserts green (115/115 archetypes, validateCityMap OK 520/640 placements), prod build 224.16KB gz (budget 240), v4 PLACEMENTS byte-identical to /tmp/v4-placements-baseline.json (449 v4 rows + 70-row v5 tail + stack-chan row = 520). Validation (agent-browser, 390x844 mobile, dev server + prod preview smoke): OPENING — floor clean at 2cm (no grey groove; also clean at r=1 street where the OSM ribbons mid-fade), parts ring the ball, first absorb in <2s of rolling (2cm→2.86cm, score 580), 🔩 parts-arrow live while score==0 then retires with glyph restored to 🗼 and no premature/eaten tower toast, Donack 「センゴク電子パーツからスタート！…」, HUD tier label センゴク電子. STACK-CHAN — collected off shelf A: popup スタックチャン NEW コレクション13/13 with cyan-faced voxel-cube thumbnail, mask appends 0xFFF→0x1FFF, old 12-bit saves load as 12/13, 13-cell grid with centered 13th cell fits mobile. AKIBA — all 6 placements alive at authored spots; found+fixed the grey-tint washout (colorHex -1 → FALLBACK_PALETTE); after fix the arcade's rainbow signage and maid cafe pink render as authored; 3 of 6 absorbed mid-game at r 16–38m. EARTH ENDING — hands-off phase log proves full pace (merge 1.2s → ascension 7.07s = GOAL_ASCEND_S 7.0 → afterglow 2.53s → done); glowing Earth limb with golden Japan-arc city lights + star field captured; Donack 「うわぁ…地球が見えるよ…！」; deliberate skip (ts=5) reaches the result screen; found+fixed the result header 13/12. REGRESSIONS — draw calls 58/72 street r=1, 50/72 marunouchi r=40, 21/72 mid-ascension (cap 72 honored); KeyR rescale invariant to 1 ulp at mid-fade r=1.5 (ws 1→5) and mid-ascension at 647m with no pop; restart cycles clean; zero console errors on dev and prod. Servers killed, browser session closed. Docs-site/GitHub updates left to the main loop per spec.
